source_pr.title: planner: update PlanBuilder.windowSpecs when building subquery
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #30804

Problem Summary:
When `PlanBuilder` enters a subquery without window function, `PlanBuilder.windowSpecs` is set to nil, which causes nil pointer dereference when handling outer window functions.

### What is changed and how it works?
Since outer named windows cannot be referred in subquery, each time we enter a subquery we save original `windowSpecs` and reset it to nil, and when we leave the subquery we restore it to the original value.


### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk.MustExec("select avg(0) over w from t1 window w as (order by (select 1))") (planner/core/integration_test.go)

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustExec("select avg(0) over w from t1 window w as (order by (select 1))") (planner/core/integration_test.go).
